enrollWizard.backupBody: They are the only way to sign in if you lose access to your authenticator. Each code works once.

documents.empty: No documents ingested yet. Upload one to get started.
documents.deleteConfirm: Delete "{{title}}" and all its chunks from the knowledge graph?

pipeline.heading: What happens after upload?
pipeline.intro: When a document is uploaded, the backend runs an asynchronous ingestion pipeline that transforms it into a searchable knowledge graph. Here are the steps:
pipeline.step1: Text Extraction — text_extractor.go extracts raw text (Gotenberg for PDFs, pass-through for .txt/.md)
pipeline.step2: Structural Parsing — structural_parser.go (PDF/TXT) or markdown_parser.go (.md) builds a hierarchical tree of sections, clauses, and articles. Cleans OCR boilerplate, promotes numbered sub-clauses (e.g. 4.4.1) to proper nodes, and detects requirement levels (SHALL / SHOULD / MAY)
pipeline.step3: Chunking — chunker.go splits the tree into chunks respecting structural boundaries. Each chunk inherits metadata: full path, numbering, node type, requirement level. Lists are kept together with their introductory clause when possible
pipeline.step4: Contextual Enrichment — contextual_enrichment.go calls the LLM to generate a short context prefix for each chunk (Contextual Retrieval). The prefix is prepended before embedding so vectors capture broader document context
pipeline.step5: Embedding — Each chunk is embedded into a vector via the configured embedding model. The embedding uses the contextualized text (prefix + chunk) for better retrieval
pipeline.step6: Graph Node Creation — ingestion_service.go creates :RagDocument, :RagSection, and :RagChunk nodes in Memgraph
pipeline.step7: Structural Relationships — ingestion_service.go creates HAS_SECTION, CONTAINS, NEXT_SECTION, and NEXT edges to encode hierarchy and reading order
pipeline.step8: Semantic Relationships — relationship_extractor.go extracts definitions (DEFINES), cross-references (REFERENCES), and computes similarity edges (SIMILAR_TO, cosine ≥ 0.85)
pipeline.step9: Indexing — Vector indexes and property indexes are created for fast retrieval
pipeline.footer: All files are located in backend/internal/rag/services/. The pipeline is orchestrated by ingestion_service.go.

deleteModal.title: Delete tenant
deleteModal.intro: You are about to soft-delete tenant {{name}}. It will stop appearing in the default tenant list and no user will be able to access it. You can still view it with the "Include soft-deleted" toggle.
deleteModal.bindingsRemain: Bindings and memberships remain in place. If you also need to crypto-shred the tenant's encryption key (GDPR right-to-erasure), open the tenant's detail modal and use the Purge action instead.

createModal.name: Name
createModal.slugHint: Lowercase letters, numbers and dashes. Auto-generated from the name unless you override it.

purgeModal.title: Purge tenant
purgeModal.alertBody: Purging crypto-shreds the tenant's KMS key. Every ciphertext sealed with that key becomes mathematically unrecoverable even if the rows themselves are restored from a backup.
purgeModal.aboutToPurge: You are about to purge tenant {{name}} ({{slug}}).
purgeModal.consequenceKms: The wrapped data-encryption key is deleted from KMS.
purgeModal.consequenceNoUndo: No undo, no grace period, no recovery path.
purgeModal.consequenceAudit: Audit-trail rows referencing the tenant are retained for regulatory reasons; the data they describe stays sealed.
purgeModal.typePrompt: Type the tenant slug below to enable the purge button:

plan.planHelp: Changing the plan pre-fills the features checklist with that plan's defaults. You can override individual features before saving.
plan.wildcardInfo: The * wildcard grants every feature — individual checkboxes have no effect while it is present.

intro.title: Privacy & your data
intro.body: Under the GDPR you have the right to access a copy of your personal data (right of access) and to request its deletion (right to erasure). Both flows run against every module that holds data about you.

export.title: Export your data
export.description: Downloads a JSON bundle with the personal data every module holds for your account. Safe to retry — the endpoint is read-only and does not mutate state.
export.toastPartial: Some modules failed to export. Open the file to review the errors section.
export.toastFailure: Failed to export your data. Please try again later.

erase.title: Delete your account
erase.description: Permanently removes your personal data across every module. You will be signed out immediately. There is no undo and no recovery path.
erase.toastFailure: Failed to erase your account. Please try again later.

warn.intro: You are about to permanently delete your account and every record tied to it. This is a GDPR right-to-erasure action — the backend runs every registered PII producer and hard-deletes the matching rows.
warn.bullet1: Your user profile, sessions, MFA factors, and OAuth bindings are wiped.
warn.bullet2: All refresh tokens are invalidated — you cannot sign back in.
warn.bullet3: Data you authored about other subjects (shared notes, audit rows, workspace edits) is retained for regulatory and audit-trail reasons.
warn.bullet4: There is no undo, no grace period, and no recovery path.
warn.stopNotifications: If you only want to stop receiving notifications, use Account → Notifications instead.

typeEmail.prompt: Type your email below to confirm the account you are deleting:

finalStep.alert: This is the final confirmation. Pressing Erase my account runs the deletion immediately.
finalStep.body: You will be signed out and redirected to the login page. You will not be able to log back in.

userSecurity.pageSubtitle: Manage your password, second factors, and active sessions.

passwordTab.title: Change password
passwordTab.ssoOnlyHint: Your account uses a single sign-on provider only. Set a password here to add a second login method.
passwordTab.minLengthHelp: At least {{count}} characters. Avoid passwords that have appeared in known breaches.
passwordTab.successToast: Password updated. Other sessions have been signed out.
passwordTab.errorMismatch: New password and confirmation do not match.
passwordTab.errorTooShort: New password must be at least {{count}} characters.

backupCodesTab.title: Backup codes
backupCodesTab.noEnrollment: Enroll an authenticator app first — backup codes are issued at enrollment and can only be regenerated for an enrolled factor.
backupCodesTab.remainingOne: You have {{count}} unused backup code. Each code works exactly once and is meant for emergency sign-in if you lose your authenticator.
backupCodesTab.remainingOther: You have {{count}} unused backup codes. Each code works exactly once and is meant for emergency sign-in if you lose your authenticator.
backupCodesTab.modalBody: Your existing backup codes will stop working immediately. Make sure you save the new codes — they are shown exactly once.

backupCodesDisplay.saveBody: They are the only way to sign in if you lose access to your authenticator. Each code works once.
backupCodesDisplay.ackLabel: I have saved these backup codes somewhere safe.

sessionsTab.title: Active sessions
sessionsTab.modalBody: Sign out every device except this one? Other browsers will need to sign in again on the next request.
sessionsTab.errorMfaRequired: Your role requires MFA. Enroll a second factor in Security settings before revoking sessions.

trustedDevicesTab.title: Trusted devices
trustedDevicesTab.empty: No devices remembered. Trust grants expire after 30 days.
trustedDevicesTab.modalBody: Every device will require MFA on its next login. Continue?

linkedProvidersTab.title: Linked sign-in providers
linkedProvidersTab.emptyHasMore: Use the “Link a sign-in” button above to add one.
linkedProvidersTab.emptyAllLinked: All supported providers have been linked.
linkedProvidersTab.onlyCredentialWarning: This is your only login method. Unlinking it would lock you out — set a password first.
linkedProvidersTab.bannerFailureGeneric: Linking did not complete. Please try again.

linkFailures.already_linked: That account is already linked to another user.
linkFailures.duplicate_provider: You already have a provider of this kind linked.
linkFailures.invalid_userinfo: The provider returned incomplete profile data.
linkFailures.internal: Something went wrong while linking. Try again.

linkedProvidersTab.errorMfaRequiredLink: Your role requires MFA. Enroll a second factor in Security settings before linking a new provider.
linkedProvidersTab.errorMfaRequiredUnlink: Your role requires MFA. Enroll a second factor in Security settings before unlinking a provider.
linkedProvidersTab.errorLastCredential: You have no other login method. Set a password before unlinking this provider.
linkedProvidersTab.modalBody: Remove the {{provider}} sign-in method? You can re-link it later from the login screen.

profileShared.errorLoad: Error loading user data. Please try again later.

adminRoles.intro: Manage roles and bindings for {{tenant}}. System roles are read-only; custom roles can be created by picking permissions from the catalog.

deleteModal.title: Delete role
deleteModal.intro: You are about to delete the custom role {{name}}. This cannot be undone.
deleteModal.warning: Bindings cascade. Any user currently granted this role will lose its permissions immediately, and the binding rows that reference it will be removed.

createModal.title: Create custom role
createModal.nameHelp: Used when binding users to the role.
createModal.descriptionPlaceholder: Short summary shown in the role list

editModal.systemInfo: System roles come from code. Their name, description, and permissions are re-seeded on every boot, so they're read-only here. You can still disable this role to prevent it from being granted to users.
editModal.activeHelp: When off, existing bindings stop granting this role's permissions and new bindings cannot be created.

bindingModal.title: Grant role to user
bindingModal.userUuidHelp: The global user UUID (from /v1/admin/users or the user profile page). A proper user picker is planned.
bindingModal.expiresHelp: Leave empty for permanent grants. Use this for contractor access, temporary elevations, or trial memberships.
bindingModal.validationRequired: Email and at least one role are required

rolesTable.errorBody: Your account needs the authz.role.read permission in this organization. If this is a fresh install and you are the first admin, try signing out and back in to refresh your token.
rolesTable.sectionSystemSubtitle: Immutable — ship with Orkestra. You can disable any of them to block new bindings, but name and permissions stay fixed.
rolesTable.sectionCustomSubtitle: Scoped to this organization. Edit permissions, disable, or delete them. Deleting cascades every binding that references the role.
rolesTable.systemEmpty: No system roles loaded. This should never happen — check backend logs.
rolesTable.noCustomRolesBody: System roles usually cover the common cases. Create a custom role when you need a narrow set of permissions for a specific team.
rolesTable.tooltipDisable: Disable this role to stop granting its permissions

bindingsTable.errorIntro: Failed to load bindings. Check that you have the authz.binding.read permission in this organization.
bindingsTable.empty: No role bindings yet. Use Grant role to user to assign a role to a specific user in this organization.

permissionPicker.heading: Permissions
permissionPicker.searchPlaceholder: Filter permissions by key, description or module…

logLevels.title: Log levels
logLevels.description: Runtime log-level admin (ADR-0005 Phase F). Per-module overrides take effect immediately — no restart. Stdout + OTLP fanout (Phase E) honor the same threshold.
logLevels.confirmReset: Reset every log level to the env defaults captured at boot? This persists the change.
logLevels.resetDoneToast: Log levels reset to boot defaults
logLevels.globalFailToast: Failed to update global log level
logLevels.loadFailed: Failed to load log levels. The logging module may have failed init — check backend logs.

metrics.noMetricsAvailable: No metrics available for this user

authMethods.passwordOauthOnly: No password set — user signs in via OAuth only.
authMethods.emailConfirmed: The user has confirmed their email address.
authMethods.emailNotConfirmed: The user has not yet confirmed their email address.
authMethods.mfaResetSelfTooltip: Use the self-service flow on your own account.
authMethods.mfaRequiredGrace: Required, no factor enrolled. Grace period ends {{date}}.
authMethods.mfaRequiredNoFactor: Required for this role; no factor enrolled yet.
authMethods.oauthEmpty: No OAuth identities are linked to this account.
authMethods.oauthBlockSelf: You cannot unlink your own OAuth identity from the admin surface.
authMethods.oauthBlockOnlyCredential: Send a password-reset first to keep the account accessible.
authMethods.unlinkConfirmBody: The user will lose this login method. They can re-link from their account settings later.
authMethods.toastUnlinkLastCredential: User has no other login method — send a password-reset first.
authMethods.toastUnlinkSelfAction: You cannot unlink your own OAuth identity from the admin surface.

roleDescriptions.developer: Technical power user — cannot manage admin/super_admin
roleDescriptions.administrator: Organization administrator — cannot elevate peers to admin

profileModal.title: Edit User Profile
profileModal.toastEmailInUse: Email already in use by another user

lockModal.confirmLock: Are you sure you want to lock the account of {{name}}?
lockModal.confirmUnlock: Are you sure you want to unlock the account of {{name}}?
lockModal.warningLock: The user will no longer be able to access the system until the account is unlocked.
lockModal.warningUnlock: The user will be able to access the system again.

createModal.title: New User
createModal.helpUsername: Username will be used for system login

mfaReset.title: Reset MFA for {{user}}
mfaReset.warning: This will delete the user's registered authenticator and backup codes. They will be forced to enroll a new factor on their next sign-in, subject to the 7-day grace window.
mfaReset.prompt: Enter your own authenticator code to authorize this action.

errors.missingCode: Enter your authenticator code to authorize the reset.
errors.noFactor: This user has no MFA factor to reset.
errors.generic: Could not reset the factor. Please try again.

aiModelsSection.title: AI Provider Configuration
aiModelsSection.unsavedBody: You have unsaved configuration changes. Are you sure you want to leave?
aiModelsSection.emptyModels: No models configured for {{provider}}. Click "Add Model" to get started.

adminModules.noMatch: No modules match the current filters.

configCard.noSettings: This module has no configurable settings.
configCard.unsavedBody: You have unsaved configuration changes. Are you sure you want to leave?

detail.saveFailed: Could not save configuration. Try again.
detail.noConfig: This module has no configurable fields.

gate.errorBody: The frontend could not contact the backend to check whether the initial setup wizard should run. Make sure the backend container is up and reachable from your browser, then retry.
gate.errorDefaultDetail: The setup probe at /v1/setup/status did not respond.

wizard.errorBody: The setup wizard could not contact the backend at /v1/setup/status. Make sure the backend container is running and reachable, then retry.

welcome.title: Welcome to Orkestra
welcome.intro: Let's get your deployment ready. This will take about a minute and needs to happen exactly once per install.
welcome.step1: Create an administrator account. The first user becomes the root developer and can manage everything else from the admin UI.
welcome.step2: Create your first organization. Orkestra is multi-tenant — every feature lives inside an organization, and you'll be enrolled as the owner.
welcome.step3: Configure outbound email. Password resets and verification links need a working SMTP relay. You can skip this step and configure it later, but those flows will silently drop mail until you do.
welcome.step4: You're done. The wizard won't reappear on the next boot.

admin.title: Create the administrator account
admin.intro: This user becomes the platform root with the developer role. You can add more users later from the admin UI.
admin.passwordHelp: Argon2id-hashed on the backend. Must be at least 10 characters and not contain your email address.
admin.errorPasswordLength: Password must be at least 10 characters.
admin.errorConflict: An administrator already exists. Reload this page to continue to the login screen.
admin.errorGeneric: Could not create the administrator account. Please try again.

org.title: Create your first organization
org.intro: Orkestra is multi-tenant — every feature lives inside an organization. We'll create one now and enroll you as the owner. You can rename it or add more organizations later from the admin UI.
org.nameHelp: Human-readable label shown throughout the admin UI.
org.slugHelp: URL-safe identifier. Auto-derived from the name until you edit it. Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only.
org.errorSlugInvalid: Slug must contain only lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens.
org.errorSlugConflict: An organization with slug "{{slug}}" already exists. Pick a different slug.
org.errorGeneric: Could not create the organization. Please try again.

smtp.title: Configure email delivery
smtp.intro: Orkestra uses these settings to send verification and password-reset email. Leave the provider on noop to skip entirely — you can configure SMTP later under /admin/modules.
smtp.noopNotice: Note: while the provider is noop, password-reset and email-verification mail is logged to the backend stdout instead of being delivered. Anyone who forgets their password will need a backend operator to recover the link from the logs.
smtp.errorHostRequired: SMTP host is required when the provider is not set to "noop".
smtp.errorFromRequired: From address is required when the provider is not set to "noop".
smtp.errorGeneric: Could not save SMTP settings. Please check the values and try again.

finish.title: Orkestra is ready
finish.bodyWithOrg: Your administrator account has been created, you're signed in, and the organization {{orgName}} is active. You can rename it or create more organizations later.
finish.bodyWithoutOrg: Your administrator account has been created, you're signed in, and the setup wizard will not appear again.
finish.smtpWarning: SMTP is not configured. Password-reset and verification mail will log to the backend stdout instead of being delivered. Configure it any time from /admin/modules — the notification module has the same fields you just skipped.
